top_signals.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
top_signals.summary: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.

top_signals.title: Nike Can’t Afford to Lose This World Cup
top_signals.summary: Nike has the star power and home advantage. But with its turnaround moving slowly, the World Cup may matter more to Nike than Adidas, explains Bloomberg Opinion columnist Andrea Felsted. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Estonia to quarantine emails sent from Russian .ru domain before they reach government officials
top_signals.summary: Estonia will require additional security screening for emails sent from Russia’s .ru top-level domain before they reach government officials, according to the country's minister of justice and digital affairs.

alerts.title: Streeting attacks Burnham’s pledges as ‘appeal to party at expense of Brits’
alerts.summary: Former health secretary Wes Streeting levelled a series of thinly-veiled attacks against Manchester mayor Andy Burnham, ahead of a crucial by-election that will likely set a Labour leadership contest in motion.  In a speech in Tower Bridge in London, Streeting appeared to criticise Burnham’s political campaign in Makerfield in the weeks leading up to polling [...]
